headline: General Mills agreed to sell €1.7 billion in aggregate principal amount of euro-denominated junior subordinated notes due 2056.
investor_impact: This €1.7 billion debt issuance extends the company's maturity profile out to 2056 while locking in fixed-to-fixed reset rates. While it increases total debt and interest expense, it represents routine capital structure management, though investors will monitor if proceeds are earmarked for refinancing or general corporate purposes.
key_figures: €1,000,000,000 aggregate principal amount of 4.750% Series A notes due 2056
€700,000,000 aggregate principal amount of 5.250% Series B notes due 2056
Barclays Bank PLC, Deutsche Bank AG, Citigroup Global Markets Limited, and J.P. Morgan Securities plc as underwriters
